ORDER ON SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE JOUNG'S PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS, CALENDAR OF FEB-09-2009 IT IS ORDERED THAT: 1.) THE TRUST IS PART OF DECENDENT PON'S TOTAL ESTATE AND IS AN AVAILABLE POOL OF ASSET FROM WHICH TO PAY CONSERVATORSHIP EXPENSES. CONSERVATORSHIP EXPENSES WHICH EXCEED CONSERVATORSHIP INCOME ARE TO BE PAID FROM THE TRUST RESIDUARY. THERE IS ADEQUATE TRUST RESIDUARY TO PAY THE CONSERVATORSHIP EXPENSES. THEREFORE, THERE IS NO NEED TO ALLOCATE CONSERVATORSHIP EXPENSES AMONG THE NON-RESIDUARY TRUST BENEFICIARIES. THE GIFTS OF "STOCKS AND BONDS" AND "BANK ACCOUNTS AND CASH" GO TO THE RESPECTIVE TRUST BENEFICIARIES, EXCLUDING JOINT TENANCY PROPERTY AND SUBJECT TO ANY COURT APPROVED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. 2.) MUTUAL FUNDS SHALL BE TREATED AS STOCKS AND BONDS FOR PURPOSES OF DISTRIBUTION UNDER THE TRUST DISTRIBUTIVE TERMS.

calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Bertha Joung (Trustee/Petitioner). Cynthia Lee for Hong Ho and Nuen Ying Yung (Objectors). Warren Woo for Myra Chan Lui. Peter Lee (Beneficiary) in propria persona.

The Court stated there was an issue as to the fourth and final account which makes a proposal of using the date of death instead of using the date the conservatorship was established. Mr. Siracusa stated that minor amendments to the account were filed, that this has been a problematic case, that when they were getting ready to close out the Trust they sent out copies to everyone, that they had consents from all counsel to proceed, that they filed the amended account, and that Ms. wouldn't sign so they filed the amendments. Ms. Lee argued that the bank accounts can be considered a specific gift and provided copies of her case cites for the Court. Mr. Woo stated that he faxed his objections to Ms. Lee last Friday, argued the specific gift issue, stated that there are other types of gifts than residual and specific, argued that there were no specific accounts or cash amounts indicated, that to make specific gifts it must be specifically identified; was the Trustor referring to accounts already opened or subsequent ones, and that he agrees with the amended fourth account. Peter Lee stated that he believed that the stocks and bonds were specific gifts. Mr. Siracusa stated that he had prepared a proposed order if the Court agrees with Mr. Lee, and that if the Court doesn't agree with their current account then he would have to prepare a new order with the Examiner's guidance.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-02-2010 Continued To Jun-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Continued To Jun-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To Mar-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Bertha Joung (Petitioner).

The Court questioned the calculations of the gifts. Ms. Alavi stated that she could amend the petition, that it was a difficult matter, that she worked with everybody, that everybody agreed to this distribution and that there was no objection. The Court suggested getting something in writing from everyone agreeing to this calculation/distribution. The matter was continued to March 3, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren Woo for Myra Chan Lui. Cynthia Lee for King Hong Ho and Nuen Ying Yung. Christopher Jew for Peter Lee and Jian Yi Hu. Avid Alavi for Bertha Joung.

The Court stated that instruction number two had been ruled on, that the parties were here in regards to instruction number one, and inquired if the parties were willing to mediate. All counsel, except Mr. Jew, agreed to mediate. Mr. Jew stated that his clients wanted the Court to enter a ruling and were not willing to mediate. The parties presented argument to support their positions. The matter was taken under submission. The Court stated that it had looked over the amendments by Mr. Woo, and returned them in open court. Ms. Alavi provided the Court with a copy of the proposed order regarding instruction number two. The Court noted that all counsel had approved Ms. Alavi's order as to form. In addition, the Court stated it was inclined to use one order, that it would either use the order originally submitted by Mr. Siracusa, or that it would make sure that the language or the intent of Ms. Alavi's order regarding instruction number two would be incorporated into its order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. McDaniels.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-02-2009 Continued To Feb-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren Woo for Myra Chan Lui. Cynthia Lee for King Hong Ho and Nuen Ying Yung. Christopher Jew for Peter Lee and Jian Yi Hu. Lawrence Siracusa for and with Bertha Joung.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the allocation percentages and the mutual funds. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court ordered the mutual funds be considered stocks. Mr. Siracusa will submit a proposed order. The Parties will consider participating in mediation on the issue of the allocation percentages. The matter was continued to February 9, 2009 for Counsel to indicate whether mediation will go forward.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.
